findings.title: Number() on env vars silently produces NaN for non-numeric input
findings.detail: daemon.ts lines 80 and 542 use Number() to parse CONTROL_PLANE_PORT and IFLEET_DAEMON_TICK_MS. If either env var is set to a non-numeric string, Number() returns NaN — the port binding silently fails and setInterval(NaN) degrades to a 1ms spin-loop. No validation or fallback is applied.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace Number() with validated parseInt: parse with parseInt(v, 10) and throw if !Number.isFinite(port) || port <= 0.

findings.title: sessionDeferred in spawn-runner.ts has no timeout — hangs if worker never emits init
findings.detail: runStreaming() resolves sessionDeferred when a worker emits an 'init' event (line 99-100) or settles it on process close (lines 161-165). If the spawned process hangs indefinitely — never emitting init and never closing — any caller awaiting handle.sessionId will block forever with no timeout or error. The AbortSignal / cancel() path only helps if the caller provides one.
findings.fix_sketch: When AbortSignal fires, also reject sessionDeferred if it hasn't settled yet.

findings.title: NO_CHANGES_NEEDED_RE can match sentinel inside markdown code blocks in editor output
findings.detail: The regex /(^|\n)\s*NO_CHANGES_NEEDED\s*($|\n)/ in pipeline/runner.ts matches whenever NO_CHANGES_NEEDED appears on its own line anywhere in the editor output, including inside a fenced code block. A false positive here causes the pipeline to short-circuit with status=already_resolved even when the task was not completed — silently dropping the finding without any fix applied.
findings.fix_sketch: Tighten regex to /^\s*NO_CHANGES_NEEDED\s*$/ and test against output.trim() so it only matches when the entire output is the sentinel.

findings.title: Dead sessionId initialization in spawn-runner.ts
findings.detail: Line 85 declares `let sessionId = ''` but this empty-string value is never used — it is always overwritten when the 'init' event fires (line 99). The initialization is a red herring that implies sessionId has a meaningful default, when in practice an unsettled sessionDeferred means no init event has arrived.
findings.fix_sketch: Change to `let sessionId: string | undefined;` and use `sessionId ?? ''` at callsites that need a string.
